description: Fix 7 remaining security vulnerabilities: WebSocket path validation, sender verification, regex centralization, method allowlist automation, frame size limits, debugger permission docs, and schema validation timeout.

userStories.title: Validate WebSocket URL path in offscreen document
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want the offscreen document to validate the WebSocket URL path (not just host) so that a compromised /ws-info response cannot redirect the connection to an arbitrary endpoint.
acceptanceCriteria: isValidWsOrigin() in offscreen/index.ts checks parsed.pathname === '/ws' after host validation
URLs like ws://localhost:9515/evil or ws://localhost:9515/ws/extra are rejected
Warning is logged when path validation fails
All existing call sites of isValidWsOrigin continue to work
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/offscreen/index.ts lines 69-88. The function currently only checks parsed.protocol and parsed.host. Add a third check: `if (parsed.pathname !== '/ws') { console.warn('[opentabs:offscreen] Rejected wsUrl with invalid path:', parsed.pathname); return false; }`. The server's WebSocket endpoint is always at /ws — hardcoded in http-routes.ts.

userStories.title: Add sender validation to offscreen chrome.runtime.onMessage
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want the offscreen document's message listener to validate that messages come from our own extension so that content scripts or other extensions cannot send ws:send messages.
acceptanceCriteria: chrome.runtime.onMessage listener checks sender.id === chrome.runtime.id at the top of the handler
Messages from other senders are silently ignored
A comment explains this is defense-in-depth
ws:send, ws:getState, and ws:setUrl message types are all protected
userStories.notes: File: platform/browser-extension/src/offscreen/index.ts line 322. The listener currently uses `_sender` (unused). Change to `sender` and add at the top: `if (sender.id !== chrome.runtime.id) return false;`. The sender parameter has type chrome.runtime.MessageSender with an `id?: string` field.

userStories.title: Centralize plugin name validation regex in browser extension
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the plugin name validation regex to be defined in one place so that inconsistencies cannot create validation bypass vulnerabilities.
acceptanceCriteria: A single VALID_PLUGIN_NAME regex is exported from a shared location (e.g., src/constants.ts)
iife-injection.ts imports it instead of defining its own SAFE_PLUGIN_NAME
message-router.ts imports it instead of using inline regex
side-panel/App.tsx imports it instead of defining its own VALID_PLUGIN_NAME
No inline plugin name regex patterns remain in any browser extension source file
The isSafePluginName function continues to also check RESERVED_NAMES
userStories.notes: Current duplication: (1) iife-injection.ts:5 defines SAFE_PLUGIN_NAME = /^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$/; (2) message-router.ts:101 inline regex; (3) message-router.ts:507 same inline; (4) side-panel/App.tsx:14 VALID_PLUGIN_NAME. Add to platform/browser-extension/src/constants.ts: `export const VALID_PLUGIN_NAME = /^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$/;` and `export const isValidPluginName = (name: string): boolean => VALID_PLUGIN_NAME.test(name);`. Import in all 4 files. The extension can't use @opentabs-dev/shared at runtime (browser context), so the constant must live within the extension.

userStories.title: Add test to verify ALLOWED_METHODS covers all methodHandlers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a test that catches when a new method handler is added to message-router.ts but ALLOWED_METHODS in offscreen/index.ts is not updated.
acceptanceCriteria: A shared constants file exports the complete set of WebSocket method names
Both ALLOWED_METHODS and methodHandlers derive from or are checked against this shared set
A unit test verifies every key in methodHandlers is present in ALLOWED_METHODS
A unit test verifies every entry in ALLOWED_METHODS either exists in methodHandlers or is in a documented exempt list (e.g., 'pong')
userStories.notes: Create platform/browser-extension/src/known-methods.ts that exports a const array of all method names. In offscreen/index.ts, import and build ALLOWED_METHODS = new Set(KNOWN_METHODS). Write a test in src/known-methods.test.ts that imports both and asserts sync. Account for 'pong' which is handled directly by offscreen ws.onmessage, not via message-router.

userStories.title: Set WebSocket maxPayloadLength in Bun.serve config
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want the MCP server to enforce a WebSocket frame size limit at the transport layer so that oversized messages are dropped before being buffered.
acceptanceCriteria: This matches the existing MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE check in extension-protocol.ts
A comment explains the 10MB matches the application-level check
userStories.notes: File: platform/mcp-server/src/index.ts lines 181-191. The websocket config currently only has open/message/close handlers. Add `maxPayloadLength: 10 * 1024 * 1024` to the websocket config object. The existing MAX_MESSAGE_SIZE in extension-protocol.ts (line 37) is also 10MB — keep them in sync.

userStories.title: Document debugger permission usage in manifest.json
userStories.description: As a security auditor, I want the debugger permission documented so that future audits don't flag it as unnecessary.
acceptanceCriteria: A brief note added to the root CLAUDE.md under the Chrome Extension section explaining the debugger permission
The note mentions: debugger is required for network capture via Chrome DevTools Protocol (Network.enable, Runtime.enable) in network-capture.ts
userStories.notes: The debugger permission IS actively used in platform/browser-extension/src/network-capture.ts: chrome.debugger.attach, chrome.debugger.sendCommand for Network.enable and Runtime.enable, chrome.debugger.detach, and chrome.debugger.onEvent.addListener. Add a brief note under an appropriate section in the root CLAUDE.md. Keep it concise.

userStories.title: Add timeout guard on Ajv schema validation
userStories.description: As a security engineer, I want tool input validation to be bounded so that pathological schemas cannot hang the MCP server.
acceptanceCriteria: The validate(args) call in mcp-setup.ts is wrapped in a timeout or complexity-limiting mechanism
Normal schemas (which complete in microseconds) are not affected
The approach is documented with a comment explaining the rationale
userStories.notes: File: platform/mcp-server/src/mcp-setup.ts around line 309. Currently `const valid = lookup.validate(args);` is synchronous. Since Ajv validation is synchronous and schemas come from plugin manifests we've already validated, a pragmatic approach is: add a maxItems/maxProperties constraint at schema compilation time to prevent combinatorial explosion. Alternatively, wrap in try-catch with a comment explaining schemas are pre-validated at discovery time. The actual risk is low since schemas come from trusted plugins.
